decisions: Theme days: choose broad themes by cognitive mode; limit to 3–5 useful themes per week; assign one theme per day (or half-day if needed).
Keep themes mutually exclusive within a day to avoid mode-switching.
Never mix high‑focus CREATIVE and high‑interaction CLIENT blocks on the same day unless split into half‑day blocks.
Protect two 50‑minute FOCUS slots on each day; on CREATIVE days dedicate both slots to drafting/editing; on CLIENT days designate one slot for prep and one for follow-up.
Make theme explicit on calendar titles and color (e.g., “MON CREATIVE — FOCUS”).
Reserve client-facing office hours on CLIENT days so ad‑hoc requests go there rather than interrupting CREATIVE days.
Use the ADMIN day to batch process micro-tasks and planning; keep CREATIVE days focused and free of admin.
Try the pattern for 2–3 weeks and then adjust counts of each theme based on actual client flow and delivery.
If a day requires mixed work due to client load, split into two half-day themes rather than switching tasks hourly.
entities: calendar entries with explicit theme and color
ADMIN day to batch micro-tasks and planning
cognitive modes: CREATIVE, CLIENT, ADMIN, STRATEGY/PLANNING, LEARNING/OPS
